assertions.title: div-by-255 replacement is exact for all inputs

steps.action: Search for div255 exhaustive test in drawing.rs
steps.observed: Found test_div255_exhaustive at line 5423 — iterates 0..=65025, asserts div255(x) == x / 255 for each

steps.action: Verify boundary test exists
steps.observed: Found test_div255_boundaries at line 5435 — checks 0, 254, 255, 256, 65025, 127, 128, 32767

steps.action: Verify div255 implementation in lib.rs
steps.observed: Found at lib.rs: pub fn div255(x: u32) -> u32 { (x + 1 + (x >> 8)) >> 8 } — matches specification exactly

evidence.description: test_div255_exhaustive exhaustively checks all 65026 values (0..=65025) with assert_eq!(div255(x), x / 255). test_div255_boundaries additionally checks edge values including 0, 254, 255, 256, 65025, and typical alpha computation 32767.

steps.action: Search for pre-clipped draw_coverage tests
steps.expected: Tests comparing pre-clipped output vs reference for various coord combinations
steps.observed: Found section 'Pre-clipped draw_coverage tests (VAL-DRAW-002, VAL-DRAW-008)' at line 5500

steps.action: Verify test_draw_coverage_unsafe_vs_reference covers coordinate combinations
steps.expected: Test cases with positive, negative, and out-of-bounds coordinates
steps.observed: Found test_draw_coverage_unsafe_vs_reference at line 5753 — tests 8 cases: normal (0,0), partial negative (-1,-1), large negative x (-500,0), large negative y (0,-500), partial clip right/bottom (6,6), single pixel (0,0,1,1), exact surface size (0,0,8,8), clip to 1x1 (7,7,2,2). Each compares byte-for-byte ref_buf vs opt_buf.

steps.action: Verify fully-outside test
steps.observed: Found test_draw_coverage_fully_outside at line 5590 — tests entirely left, above, below, and right

steps.action: Verify single pixel and zero-size edge cases
steps.observed: Found test_draw_coverage_single_pixel (line 5614) and test_draw_coverage_zero_size (line 5641)

steps.action: Verify draw_coverage implementation uses pre-clipping
steps.observed: lib.rs draw_coverage (line ~420-510): computes start_row, end_row, start_col, end_col using i64 math before the loop. Iterates only the visible range.

evidence.description: test_draw_coverage_unsafe_vs_reference creates a safe reference implementation (draw_coverage_reference) and compares byte-for-byte against the optimized draw_coverage for 8 coordinate combos including positive, negative, large negative, and out-of-bounds. Additional tests cover fully-outside, single-pixel, and zero-size edge cases.

assertions.title: Unsafe draw_coverage inner loop identical output

steps.action: Search for unsafe draw_coverage comparison test
steps.expected: Test comparing unsafe pointer-based inner loop vs bounds-checked reference
steps.observed: Found section 'Unsafe draw_coverage comparison (VAL-DRAW-003)' at line 5667

steps.action: Verify reference implementation is safe
steps.observed: draw_coverage_reference (line 5672) uses surf.set_pixel() and surf.get_pixel() — safe bounds-checked methods. No unsafe blocks.

steps.action: Verify comparison test exists and is thorough
steps.observed: test_draw_coverage_unsafe_vs_reference (line 5753) compares ref_buf == opt_buf with assert_eq! for 8 test cases, each with varying coverage values (i*37+13 % 256) on a non-zero background Color::rgb(100,50,200).

evidence.description: A safe reference implementation (draw_coverage_reference at line 5672) uses only set_pixel/get_pixel for bounds-checked access. The test creates identical surfaces, applies reference and optimized versions, and asserts byte-for-byte equality of the output buffers for 8 diverse coordinate/size combinations.

steps.action: Search for unsafe blit_blend comparison test
steps.expected: Test comparing unsafe blit_blend vs safe reference
steps.observed: Found section 'Unsafe blit_blend comparison (VAL-DRAW-004)' at line 5833

steps.action: Verify reference implementation is safe
steps.observed: blit_blend_reference (line 5808) uses surf.set_pixel() and surf.blend_pixel() — safe bounds-checked methods.

steps.action: Verify test cases cover fully in-bounds, partially clipped, and edge cases
steps.observed: test_blit_blend_unsafe_vs_reference (line 5835) tests 6 cases: fully inside (4x4 at 0,0 in 8x8), partial clip right/bottom (4x4 at 6,6), dst smaller than src (4x4 at 0,0 in 2x2), single pixel source, exact size, clip to 1x1. Source has mixed alpha (alpha = row*sw+col * 60 % 256).

steps.action: Verify opaque source fast path test
steps.observed: test_blit_blend_opaque_source (line 5892) verifies opaque source fast path produces correct output.

evidence.description: A safe reference implementation (blit_blend_reference at line 5808) uses set_pixel/blend_pixel. test_blit_blend_unsafe_vs_reference creates surfaces with non-zero background, applies both implementations with mixed-alpha sources, and asserts byte-for-byte buffer equality for 6 cases covering fully in-bounds, partial clip, dst smaller than src, single pixel, exact size, and clip-to-1x1.

steps.action: Search for unsafe fill_rect_blend comparison test
steps.expected: Test comparing unsafe fill_rect_blend vs safe reference
steps.observed: Found section 'Unsafe fill_rect_blend comparison (VAL-DRAW-005)' at line 5949

steps.action: Verify reference implementation is safe
steps.observed: fill_rect_blend_reference (line 5937) uses surf.blend_pixel() per-pixel — safe bounds-checked method.

steps.action: Verify test covers opaque, semi-transparent, and fully transparent
steps.observed: test_fill_rect_blend_unsafe_vs_reference (line 5951) tests 4 colors: semi-transparent red (a=128), quarter green (a=64), nearly transparent grey (a=1), mostly opaque white (a=200). Each is tested against 7 rect configurations including full surface, interior, partial clip, single pixel, zero width/height, and entirely outside.

steps.action: Verify opaque and transparent edge cases
steps.expected: Dedicated opaque delegation and transparent no-op tests
steps.observed: test_fill_rect_blend_opaque_delegates (line 5982) verifies opaque fast path matches fill_rect. test_fill_rect_blend_transparent_noop (line 5993) verifies transparent is a no-op.

evidence.description: A safe reference (fill_rect_blend_reference at line 5937) uses per-pixel blend_pixel. test_fill_rect_blend_unsafe_vs_reference tests 28 combinations (4 alpha levels × 7 rect configs) with byte-for-byte buffer comparison. Additional tests verify opaque delegation and transparent no-op.

steps.action: Search for blend_over div255 correctness test
steps.observed: Found section 'blend_over div255 correctness (VAL-DRAW-006)' at line 5452

steps.action: Verify test covers all required alpha values
steps.observed: test_blend_over_div255_alpha_combinations (line 5458) uses test_alphas [0, 1, 64, 127, 128, 200, 254, 255] (8 values including all required) and test_channels [0, 1, 64, 128, 200, 254, 255] (7 values). Triple-nested loop: 8 alphas × 7 src channels × 7 dst channels = 392 combinations.

steps.action: Verify assertions check correctness
steps.expected: Opaque/transparent fast paths + intermediate range check
steps.observed: Test verifies: transparent src returns dst unchanged, opaque src returns src unchanged, semi-transparent produces opaque result (a=255 for opaque dst), and result.r is between min(sr,dr)±2 and max(sr,dr)±2 in sRGB space.

steps.action: Verify blend_over uses div255
steps.observed: lib.rs blend_over (line ~97): `let da_eff = div255(da * inv_sa);` — confirms div255 is used in the blend computation.

evidence.description: test_blend_over_div255_alpha_combinations tests 392 combinations (8 alpha values × 7 src channels × 7 dst channels) including all specified alphas (0, 1, 127, 128, 254, 255) plus extras (64, 200). Verifies fast paths for a=0 and a=255, and range correctness for intermediate alphas.

assertions.title: All unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments

steps.action: Grep for all unsafe blocks/functions in drawing/lib.rs
steps.observed: Found 9 unsafe blocks in lib.rs (lines 282, 297, 468, 478, 612, 726 + nested at 339, 617, 736) and 3 unsafe functions (lines 797, 847, 891)

steps.action: Grep for all // SAFETY: comments in lib.rs
steps.expected: Each unsafe block preceded by SAFETY comment
steps.observed: Found 10 // SAFETY: comments at lines 277, 292, 339, 465, 475, 610, 617, 724, 736 — all preceding or within unsafe blocks

steps.action: Grep for all # Safety doc comments in lib.rs
steps.expected: Each unsafe fn has # Safety doc
steps.observed: Found 3 # Safety doc comments at lines 793, 842, 885 — matching the 3 unsafe functions

steps.action: Verify neon.rs unsafe functions also have SAFETY comments
steps.observed: neon.rs has 4 unsafe functions (neon_fill_row, neon_blend_4px, neon_blend_const_4px, neon_div255_u32x4), each with # Safety doc comments and internal // SAFETY: comments (15 total SAFETY annotations in neon.rs)

evidence.description: Full audit: 12 unsafe blocks/functions in lib.rs — all have // SAFETY: or # Safety comments. 4 unsafe functions in neon.rs — all have # Safety doc comments and internal // SAFETY: annotations. Total: 16 unsafe items, 16 documented. See evidence file for line-by-line details.

assertions.title: Pre-clipped draw_coverage handles large negative offsets

steps.action: Search for large negative offset tests
steps.observed: Found test_draw_coverage_large_negative_y (line 5507) and test_draw_coverage_large_negative_x (line 5546)

steps.action: Verify test_draw_coverage_large_negative_y covers the specification
steps.expected: y=-500, cov_height=520, starts at first visible row
steps.observed: test_draw_coverage_large_negative_y: surface 10×100, coverage 4×520, draws at (-1, -500). Verifies: pixel (0,0) is white (coverage row 500 mapped correctly), pixel (2,19) is white, pixel (0,20) is black (beyond visible range), pixel (3,0) is black (beyond x range). Confirms pre-clip starts at visible row 500, not row 0.

steps.action: Verify test_draw_coverage_large_negative_x covers the specification
steps.expected: x=-500, cov_width=520, starts at first visible column
steps.observed: test_draw_coverage_large_negative_x: surface 100×10, coverage 520×4, draws at (-500, -1). Verifies: pixel (0,0) is white, pixel (19,2) is white, pixel (20,0) is black. Confirms pre-clip starts at visible column 500.

steps.action: Verify pre-clipped implementation in lib.rs
steps.observed: lib.rs draw_coverage: start_row = if yi < 0 { -yi } else { 0 }; start_col = if xi < 0 { -xi } else { 0 }. For y=-500: start_row=500, so iteration begins at coverage row 500 (first visible), not row 0.

steps.action: Verify pixel-identical comparison with reference
steps.expected: Reference comparison for large negative offsets
steps.observed: test_draw_coverage_unsafe_vs_reference includes cases (-500, 0, 520, 4) and (0, -500, 4, 520) — both compared byte-for-byte against the safe reference implementation.

evidence.description: Dedicated tests for large negative y (-500 with cov_height=520) and large negative x (-500 with cov_width=520) verify that the pre-clipped path starts at the first visible row/column of the coverage buffer. Output pixel verification confirms only visible coverage pixels are rendered. Additionally, test_draw_coverage_unsafe_vs_reference includes both large negative cases in its byte-for-byte comparison against the safe reference.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 8 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All drawing scalar optimization assertions verified via code inspection of lib.rs and drawing.rs test file. Exhaustive div255 test covers full blending range, pre-clipped draw_coverage tests cover all coordinate scenarios including large negatives, unsafe inner loops all have byte-for-byte reference comparisons, all unsafe blocks have SAFETY comments.
